emails.subject: Re: Door step
emails.body: Re: Door step Dear Shawn, It is unfortunate Tom did not bring up these points when the three of us discussed this on Monday. I am unclear as to whether the work AA was planning to do on power is for their internal or external purposes as when asked directly Jennifer Stevenson said that it was for both audits (which, as you know, is frowned upon by the SEC). In fact, Stacey was not part of the Doorstep team. Additionally Risk Operations (myself) did not set up or perform the deal test. Instead, this test was set up by AA and performed by members of RAC. This can be considered a internal audit for AA's purposes. Although it seemed that we had an agreement on Monday, it now looks as if you, Tom and I need to meet again and establish some additional ground rules in order to eliminate duplication of work. Regards Shona Shawn Kilchrist 09/27/2000 05:49 PM

emails.subject: Re: Door step
emails.body: I am still talking with John Sorrells and Tom Bauer about this to see if we can get efficiencies out of this process. However, AA's first postion is that RAC is not independent and objective since they approve deals, are part of due diligence, etc.... I commit to you that I will continue to push on this and will let you know the outcome. SK From: Shona Wilson 09/28/2000 08:46 AM
